title: rubus hebecaulis subsp. lebelianus
intro: Rubus hebecaulis subsp. lebelianus, also known as Lebel's raspberry, is a subspecies of bramble native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and red to yellowish berries. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus hebecaulis subsp. lebelianus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its numerous health benefits.
